question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: parseTransactionType()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TransactionBody with DATA_NOT_SET and zero unknown fields, causing parseTransactionType() to log an error and return DATA_NOT_SET.getNumber() (0), so that the transaction is classified as type 0 and silently dropped by type-based gossip filters that do not handle type 0?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: parseTransactionType()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TransactionBody with DATA_NOT_SET and more than one unknown field, causing parseTransactionType() to log an error and return DATA_NOT_SET.getNumber(), effectively suppressing the gossip of the transaction by making it appear as an unrecognized type?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: parseTransactionType()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TransactionBody with a valid DataCase that is recognized by the protobuf schema but maps to a transaction type integer that is not in the mirror node's TransactionType enum, causing downstream gossip filters to silently drop the transaction as an unknown type?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: addContractTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction that causes contractTransactionPredicate.test(entityId) to return false for all relevant entityIds, causing addContractTransaction() to skip all contract transaction records and suppress the gossip of contract participation for the transaction?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: setEntityNftTransactionPredicate()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction where the payerAccountId equals the entityId being added via addNftTransactionEntityId(), causing the predicate in setEntityNftTransactionPredicate() (which excludes payerAccountId) to filter out the entity, suppressing the gossip of NFT transaction associations for the payer account?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: setEntityNftTransactionPredicate()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a null predicate to setEntityNftTransactionPredicate(), causing it to fall back to REJECT_ALL and silently suppress all NFT entity transaction gossip for the RecordItem, even if the caller intended to enable NFT transaction tracking?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: setEntityTransactionPredicate()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a null predicate to setEntityTransactionPredicate(), causing it to fall back to REJECT_ALL and silently suppress all entity transaction gossip for the RecordItem, effectively preventing the transaction from being associated with any entity in the mirror node database?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: doAddEntityId()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction where entityTransactionBuilder is shared across multiple entityId additions (lazy initialization), causing the builder to reuse the same consensusTimestamp, payerAccountId, result, and type for all entity transactions, potentially suppressing distinct gossip records for entities that should have different transaction metadata?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: doAddEntityId()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction that causes computeIfAbsent() in doAddEntityId() to be called with the same entityId.getId() multiple times, causing only the first entity transaction to be recorded and subsequent ones to be silently dropped, suppressing gossip of repeated entity interactions?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: nextHookContext()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a CryptoTransfer transaction with no hook calls, causing hookExecutionQueue to be null, and then trigger nextHookContext() to delegate to parent.nextHookContext(), potentially consuming hook contexts from the parent's queue and suppressing hook-triggered gossip for sibling transactions?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: nextHookContext()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a deep parent chain where hookExecutionQueue is null at every level, causing nextHookContext() to recursively traverse the parent chain until parent==null and return null, suppressing all hook execution gossip for the entire transaction chain?
